'''Sedesia''' was a high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] with harsh terrain and rough weather.

==Description==
[[File:Sybegh_Abya.png|thumb|left|[[Sybegh Abya]], a native of Sedesia]]
The planet had a tilted axis of 45 degrees, which resulted in extreme weather conditions. During the winter, nearly a sixth of the planet's surface received no sunlight. The high rotating speed and the extreme differences in [[Temperature/Legends|temperature]] resulted in violent weather conditions with sudden winds and storms. This made the use of [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcrafts]] rather impossible.<ref name="TC196" />

Most of the planet's terrain was dominated by vast steppes and tundras, and the occasional [[Forest/Legends|forest]].<ref name="TC196" />

==History==
Sedesia was colonized several [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The inhabitants settled mostly in spread-out [[Farm/Legends|farms]], smaller communities and several cities,<ref name="TC196" /> the largest being [[Besia Osurne]]. They made their living from ranching, herding and logging in the wetter regions. The harsh climate and gravity made the Sedesians self-reliant, tough and determined, but also very stubborn. Technology-wise Sedesians employed modern technology, but also relied on imported space-age and locally made industrial-age items and equipment. In the tundras, where roads were very bad, [[Strider (Sedesia)|Striders]] were still the most common method of transportation.<ref name="TC196" />

During the Galactic Civil War, Sedesia was a hotbed of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] activity. Rebel operatives and fugitives often found refuge on the outlying farms, and tonnages of [[breedtash]] meat were smuggled off-world and supplied to the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="TC196" /> This changed when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] retaliated: Under the [[Pathogen-Based Loyalty Enhancement Program]], lead by [[Doctor/Legends|Dr.]] [[Fesjo Negleem]], the Empire spread the "[[FNV-23|Gray Death]]," an artificially created virus, under the population to pacify the planet. Within two weeks, the virus infected over 99 percent of the population, killing over 35,000 inhabitants and effectively disrupting the government, military and all Rebel resistance.<ref name="TC205">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 205</ref>

After the outbreak, [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] and military [[Sedesia taskforce|personnel]] moved in and took over the planet in a completely bloodless occupation.<ref name="TC205" /> [[SAGroup (COMPOR)/Legends|SAGroup]] volunteers then distributed a [[FNA-23-B|cure]] under the auspices of humanitarian relief.<ref name="TC199">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 199</ref> For months,<ref name="TC197">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 197</ref> this quashed all Rebel sentiment on the planet before the [[Mission to Sedesia|revelation]] of the truth by Rebel agents. As a result, Sedesia revolted against the Empire. However, while initially disorganized, the taskforce swiftly responded and took over the planet by force.<ref name="TC210">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 210</ref>

==Locations==
===Besia Osurne===
{{Main|Besia Osurne}}
The only [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] of the planet was located in Besia Osurne, the largest city on Sedesia. It had a population of 250,000 people and several stockyards and processing plants for breedtash meat.<ref name="TC198">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 198</ref> It also contained the [[Cantina/Legends|tavern]] [[Trail's End]].<ref name="TC199" />

===Imperial research center===
Located half a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] outside Besia Osurne,<ref name="TC209">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 209</ref> this former veterinary center served as a research center for the Imperial [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] to develop a cure for the Gray Death plague as well as the headquarters for Dr. Fesjo Negleem.<ref name="TC203">{{JournalCite|6|The Cure}}, p. 203</ref>
